description: Top-level functional decomposition of the session-storage subsystem after migration to SQLite. Each level-1 activity maps to a C4 component (see c4.json moduleRef / ideF0Ref). A2 decomposes into A21..A23 in the embedded sub-level descriptor.

activities.title: Route Storage Call
activities.description: Per-call dispatcher that detects whether <sid>.db or <sid>/ is the source of truth and forwards to the SQLite or legacy implementation. Implements DD-9 signature compatibility.

activities.title: Operate Sqlite Store
activities.description: SQLite-backed session storage. Owns connection pool, applies WAL pragmas (DD-3), runs integrity checks and schema migrations.

activities.title: Read Legacy Directory
activities.description: Read-only access to the existing per-message / per-part directory layout. Active during the dual-track period until a session is migrated.

activities.title: Migrate Legacy Session
activities.description: Idle-time worker that performs three-stage atomic migration of one legacy session per tick (DD-7 / DR-4). Inhabits the same daemon process (DD-14).

activities.title: Inspect Session State
activities.description: Debug CLI surface (DD-12) providing list / show / check subcommands. Read-only, dual-format-aware.

arrows.label: Reader Query From TUI Or Admin Panel

activities.title: Verify Database Integrity
activities.description: Runs PRAGMA integrity_check on first open per connection (DR-3). Caches result for the lifetime of the connection. Publishes session.storage.corrupted Bus event on failure and refuses to load.

activities.title: Manage Connection Pool
activities.description: Bounded LRU pool of SQLite handles keyed by sessionID (DD-10). Closes idle connections after CONNECTION_IDLE_MS to prevent handle leak.

activities.title: Run Schema Migration
activities.description: Compares meta.schema_version vs runtime expected. Runs forward migration SQL inside a transaction (DR-5); ROLLBACK on failure leaves DB at the prior version, opens read-only.

validation_notes: Plan-builder convention is single idef0.json; MIAT skill normative spec asks for separate per-level files (a0, a1, a2). Compromise: A0 context here, A2 sub-level embedded under subDiagrams.A2 to preserve traceability without splintering files.
A1 (Router) does not decompose meaningfully — it is a single dispatch function.
A2.1 / A2.2 / A2.3 from c4.json are renamed A21 / A22 / A23 to comply with MIAT IDEF0 numbering convention (digits only).
Every activity has at least one input and one output arrow per IDEF0 normative profile §5.
AGENTS.md rule 1 (no silent fallback) modeled as an explicit Control arrow on A2.
